Patch Set 1:

Nikitas, I'm not sure if this will interact with the NRS patches, but it might, so I'm adding you as an inspector.

Patch Set 2:

Build Started http://build.whamcloud.com/job/lustre-reviews/11824/

Patch Set 2: Verified

Build Successful 
 
http://build.whamcloud.com/job/lustre-reviews/11824/ : SUCCESS

Patch Set 2:

Autotest has started the test process

Patch Set 2: Looks good to me, but someone else must approve

I think this is safe from interaction with the NRS patches; even if the future TODO that is referenced in the change is implemented, i.e.
"/* TODO: next step is to use new request for reply and don't reply on old one */"

I think it will still be safe.

I have added Liang in case he also wants to inspect and I have missed something.

Patch Set 2: Verified

Tests received by maloo, run on CentOS release 6.3/x86_64: (https://maloo.whamcloud.com/test_sessions/012e3c3c-5a6f-11e2-84d3-52540035b04c).  Ran 12 tests.  No failures.

Patch Set 2: I would prefer that you didn't submit this

(6 inline comments)
Patch Set 2: (1 inline comment)

Patch Set 2: I would prefer that you didn't submit this

Hello Mikhail.

According my opinion there cfs_list_for_each_entry() and cfs_list_add() for the exp_reg_rpcs and exp_hp_rpcs should be atomic. 

Without this there is a "window" when packet was qualified like "new" but not added into one of these lists. And if the same packet comes the issue will be observed again.

Patch Set 2:

Iurii, you mean that resend request is being processed at the same time with original one? Is that real case by any chance?

Patch Set 2: No score

Hi Mikhail.

It's difficult for me to believe that such case may be observed. 
I'm even sure that if we see packet between list_for_each() and list_add() then the issue is somewhere else. 

But a) since code grows and something may be added and b) from the logical point of view it's more correct - I prefer to have these calls under the same lock. 

According my opinion if this action doesn't require deep redesign it's better to have it atomic.

In any case this situation is something "difficult to believe" so I do not insist on this request and it's up to you and Andreas to skip it.

Patch Set 2:

Iurii, I thought maybe you know real cases about that, like original request was too late due to router problems or something else. Nevertheless, I tend to agree that logically it is more correct. I am going to update patch anyway and will incorporate your changes too.
Uploaded patch set 3.

(7 inline comments)

This is better than what currently exists so I'm not against landing it, but in the reported case of the client requests still being processed on the server for a long time, I'm not sure this will help.

The server needs to silently drop the client requests that are still being processed, and let the server return the reply to the old request into the new reply buffer.

parents.subject: LU-4322 tests: disable sanity 101a in DNE

commit.subject: LU-793 ptlrpc: allow client to reconnect with RPC in progress
commit.message:
LU-793 ptlrpc: allow client to reconnect with RPC in progress

Since not letting clients with pending RPCs to reconnect caused a lot
of problems, this patch allows such a reconnection and would only
refuse to process requests with the same xids that are already being
processed.

Bulk requests are aborted upon reconnection by comparing connection
count of request and export.

Signed-off-by: Mikhail Pershin <mike.pershin@intel.com>
Change-Id: Ic4b23a71fd288df02d1040d98867373ae06b60f6
Reviewed-on: http://review.whamcloud.com/4960
Tested-by: Jenkins
Tested-by: Maloo <hpdd-maloo@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Andreas Dilger <andreas.dilger@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Johann Lombardi <johann.lombardi@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Alexander Boyko <alexander_boyko@xyratex.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <oleg.drokin@intel.com>
